profiles.title: Profiles Editor
profiles.subtitle: Manage browser profiles with Firefox policy sets (JSON / YAML). Supported: ESR 140, Release 148.
profiles.hero_note_body: Create drafts fast, validate policy flags before saving, then export the exact profile you need in JSON or YAML.
profiles.locale_note: JSON and YAML share one workspace, one validation flow, and one export surface.
profiles.theme_note: System mode follows your operating system unless you choose a fixed theme here.
profiles.dock_heading: Everything important stays within one move
profiles.dock_body: Switch schema and mode above, then use this deck for the actions you reach for most often while editing, validating, and publishing profiles.
profiles.wizard_title: Build a browser profile through a clear step-by-step wizard
profiles.wizard_body: Move through the important setup first, jump freely between steps, and finish as soon as the essentials are ready. Export stays on the last step.
profiles.wizard_context_new: You are creating a new profile. Set the essentials first, then jump to any step you need.
profiles.wizard_context_existing: You are editing an existing profile. Open any step you need, save when ready, and export from the last step.
profiles.wizard_step_one_copy: Name, schema, editor mode, and initial baseline.
profiles.wizard_step_two_copy: Browser behavior, downloads, updates, network access, and general managed preferences.
profiles.wizard_step_three_copy: Homepage, new tab, Firefox Home cards, and Home-related preferences.
profiles.wizard_step_four_copy: Search defaults, Firefox Suggest, managed engines, and search preferences.
profiles.wizard_step_five_copy: Hardening policies plus privacy and security preferences.
profiles.wizard_step_six_copy: Extensions, integrations, Firefox Accounts controls, and sync-adjacent preferences.
profiles.wizard_step_seven_copy: Review compatibility, preserve advanced policies, and download JSON or YAML.
profiles.wizard_shell_body: This overview combines curated controls with the real Mozilla policy schema, so we can keep the flow guided without hiding advanced policies.
profiles.wizard_shell_recommended_body: High-value policies we should eventually keep as first-class controls in this step.
profiles.wizard_shell_additional_body: Policies already classified into this step, but still better handled as advanced controls.
profiles.wizard_shell_preferences_body: Existing preference sections that already fit this step and can stay alongside policy controls.
profiles.wizard_shell_raw_body: Policies we preserve through the document editor until we build dedicated UI for them.
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_recommended: No recommended policies are mapped into this step yet.
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_additional: No additional mapped policies are listed for this step yet.
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_preferences: No preference sections are attached to this step.
profiles.wizard_shell_empty_raw: This step currently does not need a raw JSON fallback bucket.
profiles.wizard_shell_inline_partial_not: Some nested fields are still preserved through raw JSON.
profiles.wizard_shell_true_map_hint: One hostname or origin per line.
profiles.wizard_shell_json_hint: Use valid JSON for nested lists or objects.
profiles.wizard_shell_json_error: Nested values must be valid JSON.
profiles.wizard_shell_nested_unsupported: Some nested fields still fall back to raw JSON.
profiles.wizard_starter_body: Choose how to begin this profile. Presets apply a managed baseline across both common and advanced policy sections.
profiles.wizard_starter_blank_copy: Use a clean document and turn on policies yourself step by step.
profiles.wizard_starter_keep_copy: Best for editing an existing profile without replacing its current baseline.
profiles.wizard_starter_basic_copy: Applies a fuller managed workstation baseline with enterprise trust, home, messaging, and extension controls.
profiles.wizard_starter_classroom_copy: Locks shared devices down further with site allowlisting, permission gates, and fewer escape paths.
profiles.wizard_starter_soc_copy: Adds stricter network, privacy, shutdown, and browser hardening defaults for higher-trust environments.
profiles.wizard_group_privacy_copy: Control telemetry, Firefox account surfaces, private windows, and password prompts.
profiles.wizard_group_lockdown_copy: Reduce advanced configuration paths and tighten risky browser capabilities.
profiles.wizard_group_updates_copy: Tune update policy, default-browser prompts, search suggestions, and download behavior.
profiles.wizard_general_policy_body: Keep the core day-to-day browser behavior aligned with what users see in Firefox Settings -> General.
profiles.wizard_sync_body: Keep the Sync-facing enterprise controls together with the add-on posture administrators usually manage nearby.
profiles.wizard_settings_search_hint: Search by control, Firefox Settings area, policy key, preference key, or preset.
profiles.wizard_settings_search_empty: No matching settings found in the wizard.
profiles.wizard_preferences_general_body: Add structured Preferences entries for Startup, Tabs, Downloads, Language, Applications, Network Settings, and Updates.
profiles.wizard_preferences_home_body: Add structured Preferences entries for Homepage, new tabs, and Firefox Home content surfaces.
profiles.wizard_preferences_search_body: Add structured Preferences entries for the Search panel, suggestions, address bar, and shortcut-like surfaces.
profiles.wizard_preferences_privacy_body: Add structured Preferences entries for tracking protection, passwords, autofill, permissions, HTTPS-Only Mode, DNS over HTTPS, and security controls.
profiles.wizard_preferences_sync_body: Add structured Preferences entries for Firefox Account surfaces and for deciding which data types sync between devices.
profiles.wizard_preferences_presets_body: Start from curated entries, then adjust the raw preference key, value, status, and type when needed.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundles_body: Apply a small, coherent group of related managed preferences at once.
profiles.wizard_preferences_known_body: Add a known key with its suggested type and, when safe, a recommended managed default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_empty: No managed preferences in this section yet.
profiles.wizard_privacy_review_body: Quick totals for managed permission categories and cookie rules, even when some of them still live in the schema shell or raw fallback.
profiles.wizard_privacy_review_permissio: Permission categories locked or blocking new requests
profiles.wizard_network_review_body: Quick totals for enterprise authentication, managed certificate trust, DNS-over-HTTPS controls, and explicit Windows SSO posture.
profiles.wizard_home_review_body: Quick totals for homepage settings, first-run overrides, Firefox Home cards, and managed user messaging surfaces.
profiles.wizard_search_review_body: Quick totals for search defaults, hidden built-in engines, managed custom engines, and Firefox Suggest controls.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_section_sta: No Firefox Home card overrides yet.
profiles.wizard_search_defaults_section_: Search suggestions are off while Firefox Suggest surfaces stay enabled
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_default: Use true/false, numbers, or text. JSON objects and arrays are also accepted when value type is inferred.
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_clear: Clear removes the managed value, so no explicit value is needed.
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_known: Known preference with a suggested managed default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_hint_known_m: Known preference with multiple preset variants. Set status and value explicitly.
profiles.wizard_preferences_error_value: Preference value is required unless status is clear.
profiles.wizard_preferences_error_boolea: Boolean values must be true or false.
profiles.wizard_preferences_error_number: Number values must be valid numeric input.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_resto: Uses browser.startup.page = 3 so Firefox reopens the last session.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_open_: Uses browser.startup.page = 1 to open the configured homepage at launch.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_warn_: Keeps the standard confirmation when a user closes many open tabs.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_last_: Keep window open after the last tab
Turns off closing the whole window when the final tab is closed.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_downl: Keeps downloads in the configured folder instead of prompting every time.
Always ask where to save files
Forces Firefox to ask for a download location each time.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_smoot: Turns smooth scrolling on in the General panel.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_spell: Enables spellcheck in multi-line text areas such as comment forms.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_home_: Enable Firefox Home on new tabs
Keeps the built-in Firefox Home experience available on new tabs.
Displays the top sites strip in Firefox Home.
Shows recent and bookmarked highlights on Firefox Home.
Turns off Pocket stories on Firefox Home.
Removes sponsored tiles from Firefox Home.
Turns off small promotional snippets on Firefox Home.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundle_home_: Keeps search on, but removes stories, sponsored tiles, and snippets from Firefox Home.
Shows Top Sites with two rows and no sponsored tiles.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_searc: Allows provider suggestions in the search field and address bar.
Shows bookmark matches in address bar suggestions.
Shows history matches in address bar suggestions.
Suggest open tabs in address bar
Shows already-open tab matches while the user types in the address bar.
Suggest top sites in address bar
Keeps top-site suggestions visible in the address bar results.
Lets single-word address bar entries fall back to search.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_priva: Sends the Do Not Track signal with browsing requests.
Turns off built-in password saving through Preferences.
Stops Firefox from storing and suggesting form history.
Forces Firefox to upgrade navigations to HTTPS where possible.
Sets network.trr.mode = 2 for managed DNS over HTTPS.
Denies geolocation permission prompts by default.
Denies camera permission prompts by default.
Denies microphone permission prompts by default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundle_permi: Blocks location, camera, microphone, and notification prompts by default.
profiles.wizard_preferences_bundle_doh_c: Switches Firefox to TRR-only mode and keeps enterprise roots available.
profiles.wizard_preferences_preset_sync_: Keeps bookmark sync enabled for signed-in devices.
Keeps browsing history sync enabled for signed-in devices.
Keeps open-tab sync enabled across devices.
Disables password sync even when Firefox Accounts are available.
profiles.wizard_homepage_body: Configure the initial Firefox landing experience and lock it when the profile should stay consistent.
profiles.wizard_homepage_additional_hint: One URL per line. These become Homepage.Additional.
profiles.wizard_homepage_locked_copy: Prevents users from changing the homepage configuration in Firefox.
profiles.wizard_search_body: Set the search bar layout, suggestions, and the built-in engines your users should see first.
profiles.wizard_search_remove_hint: One engine name per line. These values become SearchEngines.Remove.
profiles.wizard_search_add_body: Build SearchEngines.Add entries visually. Existing unknown keys are preserved unless you remove the card.
profiles.wizard_search_preset_docs_copy: Template for an internal documentation search endpoint.
profiles.wizard_search_preset_ticket_cop: Starts a managed search engine for helpdesk or issue tracking.
profiles.wizard_search_preset_wiki_copy: Good starting point for an internal wiki or knowledge base.
profiles.wizard_search_preset_ddg_copy: Ready-to-edit public web search preset with a simple GET query.
profiles.wizard_search_add_empty: No custom search engines yet. Add one when you need a managed engine.
profiles.wizard_search_engine_summary_de: GET by default. Add the required fields to complete this engine.
profiles.wizard_search_engine_warning_re: Required for a valid engine: Name and Search URL template.
profiles.wizard_newtab_body: Tune the new tab page and the extra pages Firefox shows on first run or after an upgrade.
profiles.wizard_override_first_run_hint: Mozilla allows multiple URLs here, separated with a vertical bar.
profiles.wizard_firefox_home_body: Choose which discovery cards appear on the built-in Firefox Home experience.
profiles.wizard_firefox_suggest_body: Control the recommendation surfaces that Firefox can show in the address bar.
profiles.wizard_proxy_body: Set the connection mode first, then fill only the fields that matter for that mode.
profiles.wizard_proxy_passthrough_hint: Enter one hostname per line or comma-separated. These values become Proxy.Passthrough.
profiles.wizard_proxy_locked_copy: Prevents users from changing proxy configuration in Firefox.
profiles.wizard_proxy_use_http_all_label: Use HTTP proxy for all protocols
profiles.wizard_proxy_use_http_all_copy: Applies the HTTP proxy to SSL, FTP, and SOCKS traffic where appropriate.
profiles.wizard_proxy_auto_login_copy: Lets Firefox automatically authenticate to the proxy with the current user context.
profiles.wizard_proxy_use_dns_copy: Routes DNS through the proxy when SOCKS v5 is in use.
profiles.wizard_extensions_body: Manage the default install posture and the core add-on lists without leaving the wizard. One item per line for URLs and add-on IDs.
profiles.wizard_extensions_review_body: Quick totals for curated profile rules, arbitrary ExtensionSettings entries, and custom install URLs.
profiles.wizard_bookmarks_review_body: Quick totals for bookmark entries, managed bookmark folders, and rows that already carry nested bookmark trees.
profiles.wizard_website_access_review_bo: Quick totals for blocked sites, explicit WebsiteFilter exceptions, and Handler mappings that still live in the advanced shell.
profiles.wizard_extensions_profiles_body: Configure known extension IDs directly through structured controls. Custom per-ID rules can still live in the raw editor below.
profiles.wizard_extension_profile_update: Keeps this extension pinned unless you update it explicitly.
profiles.wizard_extension_profile_privat: Sets whether this extension is enabled in private windows.
profiles.wizard_extensions_summary_body: Install URLs add managed add-ons. Locked IDs keep an add-on installed. Uninstall IDs remove known add-ons from the profile.
profiles.wizard_extensions_install_hint: One URL or file path per line. These values become Extensions.Install.
profiles.wizard_extensions_locked_hint: One add-on ID per line. These values become Extensions.Locked.
profiles.wizard_extensions_uninstall_hin: One add-on ID per line. These values become Extensions.Uninstall.
profiles.wizard_extensions_advanced_body: Use the full ExtensionSettings and install-permission policies for arbitrary add-on IDs, install sources, blocked messages, and other advanced fields. These controls stay in sync with the simpler extension fields above.
profiles.sidebar_hint: Select a profile or start a new draft. Filters update the list instantly.
profiles.workspace_overview_hint: Current context, schema, and editing mode at a glance.
profiles.helper_no_selection_title: Choose a profile from the library to open it
profiles.helper_no_selection_body: Click any profile card on the left. Once opened, its metadata appears here and its browser policies load into the document editor below.
profiles.helper_selected_body: Update owner and description here, then edit the Firefox policies in the document editor below and save the result.
profiles.details_hint: Metadata lives separately from the raw policy payload, which makes the form easier to scan.
profiles.actions_hint: Choose schema, switch editor mode, validate changes, then save or export.
profiles.workflow_body: Use the form for profile metadata and the editor for Firefox policy flags. Save creates a new profile, while updates keep the original name stable.
profiles.guide_step_one_body: Set a clear name, owner, and short description before you move into raw policy editing.
profiles.guide_step_two_body: The command deck above keeps validation, formatting, saving, and recovery in one predictable place.
profiles.guide_step_three_title: Export only when the workspace is green
profiles.guide_step_three_body: Once the draft is valid and saved, use the schema-aware export links to download JSON or YAML.
profiles.flow_support_body: Keep the library on the left for navigation, the details form for metadata, and the document editor for policy payloads. That separation keeps editing fast and safe.
profiles.editor_section_hint: Monaco handles JSON or YAML editing with formatting and validation shortcuts.
profiles.editor_note: One structured editor, one validation route, and direct exports to /api/export/profiles.
profiles.status_hint: Latest system feedback for the current workspace.
profiles.description_placeholder: Short operational note for this profile
profiles.name_hint: Choose a unique name for a new profile draft.
profiles.name_locked: Profile name is locked after creation.
profiles.policy_disable_accounts_copy: Removes Firefox Account sign-in and sync entry points.
profiles.policy_disable_pocket_copy: Removes Pocket integration from the browser.
profiles.policy_password_manager_copy: Keeps the built-in password manager available to users.
profiles.policy_block_about_profiles_cop: Prevents users from switching or creating profiles in the browser UI.
profiles.policy_disable_devtools_copy: Blocks access to the built-in browser developer tools.
profiles.policy_disable_pdf_viewer_copy: Forces PDF handling outside Firefox instead of the embedded viewer.
profiles.policy_https_only_copy: Upgrades navigation to HTTPS whenever Firefox can do so safely.
profiles.policy_disable_app_update_copy: Stops Firefox application updates from being installed.
profiles.policy_disable_system_addon_upd: Prevents Mozilla system add-ons from updating in the background.
profiles.policy_save_logins_copy: Controls whether Firefox offers credential saving.
profiles.policy_prompt_downloads_copy: Prompts the user to choose a folder for each download.
profiles.policy_search_suggest_copy: Lets Firefox show provider suggestions while the user types in search.
profiles.wizard_export_review_body: Check whether the current editor state matches what the export links will download, then save or validate right here if needed.
profiles.wizard_export_validation_pendin: Validate before export if you want a fresh API check.
profiles.wizard_export_state_unsaved_exi: Unsaved changes are not in the export yet. Save first.
profiles.wizard_export_state_invalid_dir: Fix invalid JSON/YAML before saving. Export links still point to the last saved version.
profiles.wizard_export_state_ready: Saved version is ready for JSON and YAML download.
profiles.wizard_export_state_archived: Archived profiles cannot be exported until restored.
profiles.wizard_export_ready_create: Create and save this profile first, then download JSON or YAML.
profiles.wizard_export_ready_saved: The saved version below matches the editor and is ready for JSON or YAML download.
profiles.wizard_export_compatibility_bod: See how much of the current document is covered by mapped UI, how much still relies on raw fallback, and whether deprecated or unknown keys still need attention.
profiles.wizard_export_ready_body: Save the profile first if needed, then export the current version in JSON or YAML.
profiles.wizard_finish_invalid: Fix invalid JSON or YAML before finishing.
profiles.wizard_finish_name_required: Profile name is required before finishing.
profiles.wizard_network_applied: Landing, search, and proxy settings updated.
profiles.empty_list: No profiles match the current filters.
profiles.list_click_hint: Click to load this profile into the workspace.
profiles.list_selected_hint: This profile is open in the workspace.
profiles.confirm_soft_delete: Soft-delete this profile? You can restore it later.
profiles.confirm_hard_delete: Dangerous operation: this profile will be permanently deleted without recovery. Continue?
profiles.confirm_reset_library: Dangerous operation: all profiles will be permanently deleted without recovery. Continue?
